reforms.description: Iceland introduced a science-based, market-driven limit on fishing to place the industry on a sustainable basis, which includes a resource tax to ensure the industry pays for fisheries management and research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to ensure sustainable fishing practices, which can enhance long-term productivity and resource management.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a market-driven limit and resource tax represents a significant structural change in fisheries management.
reforms.source_quote: Iceland introduced a science-based, market-driven limit on fishing in time to place the industry on a sustainable basis.

reforms.description: The Icelandic New Energy Company is piloting an experiment to use hydrogen fuel cells for transportation, involving collaboration with the government and major international energy producers to test the viability of hydrogen as a clean alternative to fossil fuels.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to explore clean energy alternatives, which can lead to innovation in the energy sector and reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been publicly announced and is in the planning stages.
reforms.importance_rationale: The pilot project represents a significant step towards exploring new energy technologies, although it is still in the experimental phase.
reforms.source_quote: The Icelandic New Energy Company, with the involvement of the Icelandic government and major international energy and vehicle producers, is piloting a special experiment in Iceland to use hydrogen fuel cells.
